message:
perf(search/canonicalize): ASCII/no-markdown fast path bypasses full pipeline (ppy2e)

FILE: src/search/canonicalize.rs

Follow-up to swf6u. canonicalize_for_embedding now short-circuits through a
cheap whitespace-only path when the input is pure ASCII and contains none of
the markdown discriminators (`` ` ``, `*`, `_`, `#`, `[`). For the dominant
tool-output message shape this skips the full DefaultCanonicalizer pipeline —
NFC normalization, markdown line-by-line stripping, code-block collapse —
and just does split_whitespace + join(' ') + low-signal filter + truncate.

The fast path is a superset-preserving refinement: every input that triggers
it produces byte-identical output to the slow path. Enforced by a new test
canonicalize_fast_path_matches_slow_path_for_pure_ascii_inputs (23 cases
including markdown, non-ASCII, emoji, and low-signal acks — all asserted
equal between combined output and slow-path output). Plus
canonicalize_fast_path_truncates_to_max_embed_chars covers the truncation.

LOW_SIGNAL_CONTENT is duplicated in cass rather than re-exported from
frankensearch; drift is caught by the equivalence test. Per the comment,
any divergence from upstream shows up as a test failure before production.

VERIFICATION:
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cass_pane2 cargo test
--lib search::canonicalize: 28 tests pass including both new fast-path
tests and all 14 pre-existing tests (unicode, markdown, code blocks, list
markers, emoji, truncation, low-signal filtering).

EXPECTED WIN:
Skips NFC unicode::nfc allocation + strip_markdown_and_code line iteration
+ normalize_whitespace char-by-char loop on plain ASCII inputs. Hot path
for every indexed message and every search hit that goes through semantic
lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
